lines: Callum sits top of the sweepstake like the smug cunt he is — France hammered Iraq 3-0 and Norway beat Senegal 3-2, meaning he's basically getting paid twice every time a ball hits the net in Group I.

Speaking of Senegal getting battered 3-2 by Norway, Dene is now sitting on 7 points with a team whose best squad member is probably the kit man — Bosnia, Tunisia and Senegal have collectively managed the defensive resilience of a wet paper bag, you absolute disaster of a punter.

Ellis is cruising quietly with Argentina's perfect 6 points, which means she's either a footballing genius or just got obscenely lucky — either way, the lads should be fuming that a woman is making them look like idiots.

Today's Group K clash between Colombia and DR Congo is basically a Carter civil war — the man owns both teams and will somehow find a way to be disappointed regardless of the result, which is a special kind of sweepstake hell.

Keep your eyes on Al in Group L today — Panama vs Croatia is a genuinely grim 0-0 kind of fixture, and Al owns BOTH teams, so he's guaranteed points but also guaranteed to watch ninety minutes of absolute shite.
